seo.title: Building RAG Pipeline with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build rag pipeline with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building a RAG pipeline with OpenAI Agents SDK enables multi-agent orchestration for document retrieval, context assembly, and LLM synthesis with seamless handoffs. Guardrails are essential to prevent agent loops, prompt injections, unvalidated tool outputs, and tracing gaps that plague production systems. Vex integrates as output guardrails to ensure source faithfulness, hallucination detection, context relevance, and citation accuracy in high-risk scenarios.

architectureOverview.heading: RAG Pipeline Architecture with OpenAI Agents SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The RAG pipeline leverages OpenAI Agents SDK's multi-agent capabilities: a Router Agent triages queries, a Retrieval Agent performs vector search on document embeddings, a Context Assembler Agent chunks and ranks relevant passages, and a Synthesizer Agent generates responses with source attribution. Agents hand off via the SDK's structured handoff protocol, with built-in tracing for observability. This modular design scales but introduces risks in inter-agent communication and tool output validation.

Vector search uses embeddings from OpenAI models stored in a vector DB like Pinecone. Retrieved chunks are validated for relevance before synthesis, where the final agent cites sources using metadata. Vex guardrails wrap each agent's output to enforce RAG-specific verifications before handoffs or final response.

risks.name: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
risks.description: Router endlessly hands off between Retrieval and Context Assembler due to poor termination conditions
risks.mitigation: Vex max_iterations guardrail on agent runs

risks.name: Guardrail bypasses via prompt injection
risks.description: Malicious queries injected into RAG pipeline bypass retrieval limits or force hallucinated citations
risks.mitigation: Vex input sanitization + output faithfulness checks

risks.name: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
risks.description: Retrieval Agent returns irrelevant chunks that poison Synthesizer Agent's context
risks.mitigation: Vex context_relevance guardrail on tool outputs

risks.name: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
risks.description: Lost visibility into which agent introduced hallucination or citation error
risks.mitigation: Vex structured tracing with agent span correlation

implementation.title: Basic RAG Agents Setup
implementation.description: Initialize OpenAI Agents with Router, Retrieval, and Synthesizer for core pipeline
implementation.code:
from agents import Agent, Runner
from openai import OpenAI

router = Agent(
    name="Router",
    instructions="Triage to retrieval or direct synthesis",
    model="gpt-4o",
    handoffs=[retrieval_agent, synthesizer_agent]
)

retrieval_agent = Agent(
    name="Retriever",
    instructions="Search vector DB for relevant docs",
    tools=[vector_search_tool]
)

synthesizer_agent = Agent(
    name="Synthesizer",
    instructions="Synthesize answer with source citations"
)

runner = Runner(agents=[router])

implementation.title: Vector Search Tool Implementation
implementation.description: Pinecone vector search tool for Retrieval Agent with metadata extraction

implementation.title: Context Assembly Agent
implementation.description: Agent ranks and chunks retrieved documents for optimal synthesis context
implementation.code:
context_assembler = Agent(
    name="Context Assembler",
    instructions="""Rank docs by relevance score. Chunk to < 4000 tokens.
    Format as: SOURCES: [{source, score, chunk}]\n\nCONTEXT: {assembled}""",
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
    handoffs=[synthesizer_agent]
)

def rank_chunks_tool(docs: List[Dict]) -> str:
    # LLM reranking logic
    ranked = llm_rerank(docs)
    context = assemble_context(ranked[:3])
    return context

implementation.title: Synthesizer with Citation Format
implementation.description: Final agent generates response with inline citations referencing source metadata
implementation.code:
synthesizer_agent = Agent(
    name="Synthesizer",
    instructions="""Answer using only provided CONTEXT. Cite sources inline as [1], [2].
    At end: SOURCES:\n[1] source1 (score)\n[2] source2 (score)""")

async def run_rag_pipeline(query: str):
    result = await runner.run(query)
    return result.final_output

vexIntegration.title: Vex Output Guardrail on Retrieval Agent
vexIntegration.description: Validate tool outputs for context relevance before handoff to assembler
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Guardrail, RunContext

retrieval_guardrail = Guardrail(
    name="context_relevance",
    verifications=[
        "context_relevance>0.7",
        "no_prompt_injection"
    ]
)

@retrieval_guardrail.wrap
async def safe_retrieval(ctx: RunContext, query: str):
    docs = await vector_search_tool(query)
    # Vex validates before returning to agent
    return docs

retrieval_agent = Agent(
    tools=[safe_retrieval]
)

vexIntegration.title: Source Faithfulness Guardrail on Synthesizer
vexIntegration.description: Ensure final response faithfully uses cited sources without hallucination
vexIntegration.code:
synthesis_guardrail = Guardrail(
    name="rag_faithfulness",
    verifications=[
        "source_faithfulness>0.9",
        "hallucination_score<0.1",
        "citation_accuracy"
    ],
    risk_profile="high"
)

@synthesis_guardrail.wrap
async def safe_synthesis(ctx: RunContext, context: str, query: str):
    response = await synthesizer_agent.run(f"{context}\n\nQuery: {query}")
    return response

runner = Runner(guardrails=[retrieval_guardrail, synthesis_guardrail])

vexIntegration.title: Handoff Loop Prevention Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Prevent infinite agent handoffs with iteration limits and loop detection
vexIntegration.code:
loop_guardrail = Guardrail(
    name="handoff_loop_prevention",
    verifications=[
        "max_iterations<5",
        "no_loop_detected"
    ],
    tracing_enabled=True
)

router = Agent(
    name="Router",
    guardrails=[loop_guardrail],
    handoffs=[retrieval_agent, context_assembler]
)

# Full pipeline with Vex tracing
async def secure_rag(query: str):
    with loop_guardrail.trace() as span:
        result = await runner.run(query)
        span.set_tags({
            'rag_sources': len(result.context.sources),
            'citations': result.citations
        })
    return result
